description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a symmetrical design, featuring two large, curved blades extending from either side of a central shaft. The blades are metallic, darkened with a reddish tint and decorated with ornate engravings, giving the axe a menacing and ceremonial appearance. The handle is straight, cylindrical, and appears to be made of wood or a wood-like material, capped at both ends with metal fittings. The top of the axe is adorned with a pointed, gem-like finial, adding to its decorative and possibly symbolic function. The overall construction suggests a blend of functionality and display, suitable for both combat and ornamental use. The axe shows signs of wear and dark coloration, possibly indicating age or use in dramatic settings.
description: A double-headed, ornate battle axe with dark, engraved blades and a straight, capped handle, featuring a pointed finial on top. The design is both functional and decorative, with a menacing, ceremonial appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing both large, engraved blades and the full length of the straight handle with metal caps.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the axe, with the blades appearing as narrow edges and the pointed finial clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the symmetrical blades and handle, with engravings visible on the blade surfaces.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and the alignment of the handle and finial.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical handle, likely wood, capped with metal at both ends, no visible grip texture, round cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, symmetrical, wide crescent blades with ornate engravings, no beard, pointed finial at the top, no poll.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with metal caps securing the blade to the handle.
